sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("12-pixel objects:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] == 12:
        cr = sum(b[0] for b in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        cc = sum(b[1] for b in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        print(f"  ({cr},{cc}) color={n['color']}")

# Check orange object
print("\nOrange objects:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'O' and n['pixels'] > 5:
        cr = sum(b[0] for b in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        cc = sum(b[1] for b in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        print(f"  ({cr},{cc}) color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

So clicking on trail segments just moves the white border along the trail. I need to figure out how to remove the remaining gray-bordered objects.

3. Clicked on (23,37) - white border moved to (23,37), nothing removed
So clicking on bordered objects removes another object, but clicking on trail segments just moves the white border.

Looking at the trail structure, the white border can move from (23,37) to:
- (11,34) via trail (23,37) -> ... -> (13,35) -> (11,34)
- (23,41) via trail (23,37) -> (23,41)
